channel.title: Blind Embossing on Ric - An unknown artist
channel.description: Recent content in Blind Embossing on Ric - An unknown artist

item.title: Another version of the "JCB" blind stamp
item.description: Image by Christian Wolf - taken from Wikimedia Commons This is the probably older version of the blind stamp that’s used on some prints by Fritz Neumann. It imitates the Holsten Gate in Lübeck (second image), another indication of a North German origin. So far, inquiries from the Lübeck City Archives about former art publishers in the city have not yet been answered. For examples of the blind stamp in use, see:

item.title: Sailing boats (Ric 06)
item.description: Short description: Sailing Boats Notes: Sold originally in Paderborn, see label. Other entries for this work

item.title: More sailing boats (Ric 05)
item.description: Short description: Sailing Boats Notes : Taken from eBay . Other entries for this work

item.title: A german shepherd (Fritz Neumann)
item.description: Short description: A German Shepherd Notes: Bought in Göttingen.

item.title: Large sailing ship by Ric (Ric 25)
item.description: Short description: Large sailing ship Notes : Other entries for this work

item.title: Sailing boats (Ric 10)
item.description: Short description: Sailing Boats Notes: Taken from eBay . Other entries for this work

item.title: Sailing boats (Ric 07)
item.description: Short description: Sailing boats Notes: Slightly different colouring then other versions . Other entries for this work

item.title: A stair alley (Ric 14)
item.description: Short description: A stair (alley) Notes: Better quality then the first entry for this . Other entries for this work

item.title: A dachshund (Fritz Neumann)
item.description: Short description: A dachshund Notes: Taken from Cofart.be . The stamp mark is clearly visible.

item.title: Two dogs - fox terrier (Fritz Neumann)
item.description: Short description: Two dogs, fox terrier. Notes: Taken from eBay . This one is quite interesting, it’s attributed to Hermann Fritz Neumann and should be quite old. It also features the stamp mark, but in another form: The letters “J”, “C” and “B” are not overlapping, and are put in the middle of the Holstentor .

item.title: Sailing boats in a harbour (Fritz Neumann)
item.description: Short Description: Sailing boats in a harbour / near a shore Notes: Another maritime subject by Fritz Neumann, on the back is a sticker reading “Echt Aquarell” (Original aquarelle). But this is clearly a colourised print, since there is a platemark.

item.title: This is the stamp mark used on the prints of Ric
item.description: This is the stamp mark (blind stamp) used on the prints of Ric and Fritz Neumann. There is now a dedicated page on this trace. It will be updated in the next few days with more examples and traces. You can also look at the pages tagged with “Blind embossing” .

item.title: Sailing boat (Ric 05)
item.description: Short description: Sailing boat Notes: See Antik Bayreuth Other entries for this work

item.title: Sailing boats (Ric 06)
item.description: Short description: Sailing Boats Notes: Sold originaly in Bremerhaven, see label. The shop still seem to exist. Other entries for this work

item.description: Short description: Sailing boats Notes: Other entries for this work
